headline: Québec lève le voile sur un stratagème illégal pour rémunérer une conseillère à Scotstown
distinctive_facts: Council paid 3832.50$ indirectly to a municipal councillor's spouse via a fake invoice for 127 hours and 45 minutes of landscaping work from May 5 to September 29, 2025.
Resolution adopted with false information claiming the spouse performed planting, aesthetic development, and maintenance for the Intergenerational Park.
Councillor performed tasks reserved for employees: created park plans, selected materials, and did manual work.
Mayor failed to denounce the scheme and omitted surveillance duties.
Councillor reimbursed all sums during the investigation; no judicial action due to lack of personal gain intent.
CMQ recommendations: deposit report at council meeting and amend ethics code to prohibit council interference in daily administration.
Mayor Sylvie Dubé stated: 'Tout ce que je peux faire et ce que le conseil va faire, c'est de faire en sorte que ça ne se reproduise plus.'
CMQ expects follow-up on corrective measures by May 1.

headline: Souveraineté culturelle : Lacombe souligne « une victoire pour le Québec » à l'UNESCO
distinctive_facts: The intergovernmental committee recommends adoption of the protocol at the Conference of Parties in one year.
Minister Lacombe participated in the 19th session of the committee in Paris last week.

Lacombe's quote: 'Nous avons réussi à convaincre les 24 pays membres du comité intergouvernemental qu'il s'agit du bon outil, si bien que le comité a officiellement recommandé son adoption lors de la Conférence des parties, dans un an!'
Lacombe's campaign at UNESCO has been ongoing for two years.
Lacombe thanked federal Minister Marc Miller for rallying to the Quebec consensus despite recent divergences.
Miller's communiqué on Thursday defended Canadian cultural sovereignty in the digital era and called for international cooperation.
Formal adoption process: Conference of Parties, Executive Board, then UNESCO General Conference in fall 2027.

headline: Les résidents de la région de Saint-Jean paralysés par la neige
